attributes.description: Alerts when a StatefulSet has fewer ready pods than desired. StatefulSets manage stateful applications with stable identities, so missing replicas can cause data availability issues.
attributes.name: [K8s OTel] StatefulSet replicas not ready

investigation_guide.blob:
## StatefulSet Replicas Not Ready

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected StatefulSet(s) from the alert results.
2. Check the Workload Health dashboard for StatefulSet status.
3. Run `kubectl describe statefulset <name> -n <namespace>` for events.
4. Check individual pod status: `kubectl get pods -l app=<statefulset> -n <namespace>`.
5. Verify PVC bindings — StatefulSet pods require their PVCs to be bound before starting.

### Common Causes
- PersistentVolumeClaim binding failures (no matching PV available)
- Pod crash loops in stateful workloads
- Ordered startup blocked by earlier pod failures (StatefulSets start sequentially)
- Resource constraints preventing pod scheduling

### Tuning
- The rule filters out StatefulSets scaled to zero (`desired > 0`).
- StatefulSets roll out pods sequentially, so transient gaps during updates are normal. The `alertDelay` of 3 runs helps avoid false positives.

### Escalation
StatefulSet failures often affect databases and message queues. Prioritise investigation to prevent data loss.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-k8sclusterreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE k8s.statefulset.desired_pods IS NOT NULL
    AND k8s.statefulset.ready_pods IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    desired = LAST(k8s.statefulset.desired_pods, @timestamp),
    ready = LAST(k8s.statefulset.ready_pods, @timestamp)
    BY k8s.statefulset.name, k8s.namespace.name
// Exclude StatefulSets scaled to zero
| WHERE desired > 0
// Alert when ready pods are fewer than desired
| WHERE ready < desired
| EVAL not_ready = desired - ready
| KEEP k8s.namespace.name, k8s.statefulset.name, desired, ready, not_ready
| SORT not_ready DESC
